text: The Automatic Parking Assist (APA) system shall detect available parking spaces using ultrasonic sensors with a minimum detection range of 4.5 meters and a lateral resolution of ±10 cm.

The driver shall continuously monitor the vehicle environment and be prepared to take over control immediately during APA operation. The system shall issue a visual and auditory warning within 500ms if driver attention is lost.

The parking trajectory computation shall complete within 200 milliseconds of slot detection to comply with real-time control loop requirements. REQ-001 must be satisfied before this function activates.
The Remote Parking Assist (RPA) function shall conform to ISO 26262 ASIL-B requirements for all safety-relevant software components, including the collision avoidance and trajectory planning modules.

The 360-degree camera system (Camera360) shall provide surround-view coverage with a minimum resolution of 1280x720 pixels at 30 fps and a horizontal field-of-view of at least 190 degrees per camera unit.
The Summon function shall only operate when the vehicle speed is below 10 km/h. The system shall automatically disengage and apply emergency braking if this threshold is exceeded by more than 2 km/h.
The parking ECU shall implement redundant sensor fusion combining ultrasonic sensor and Camera360 data to achieve a pedestrian detection false-negative rate below 0.1% per operational hour.
The APA interface shall provide the driver with real-time visual feedback of the detected parking space boundary overlay on the Camera360 surround-view image. REQ-005 shall be satisfied first.
All parking functions shall maintain a maximum lateral positioning error of ±5 cm relative to the target parking slot centerline, measured under nominal environmental conditions (dry road, daylight).

The system shall log all sensor data, ECU states, driver interactions, and system decisions during parking maneuvers with a timestamp accuracy of ±1 ms for post-hoc analysis and ISO 26262 certification evidence.
The APA system shall detect parking slot type (parallel, perpendicular, angled) with a classification accuracy of at least 98% under conditions defined in REQ-001.
The parking trajectory planner shall compute a collision-free path using a minimum turning radius of 5.8 meters, consistent with the vehicle's kinematic constraints. REQ-003 applies.
The Remote Parking Assist function shall not operate if the wireless communication link between the mobile device and the vehicle has a latency greater than 100ms or a packet loss rate above 2%.
The parking system shall comply with ISO 26262 Part 6 (Software Level) requirements for safety-related embedded software, including MISRA-C coding guidelines and MC/DC test coverage of at least 90%.
The APA end-to-end maneuver duration shall not exceed 90 seconds for perpendicular parking and 120 seconds for parallel parking, measured from slot detection to engine-off.
The system shall detect static obstacles within the parking corridor using ultrasonic sensor data and halt the maneuver within 150ms of detecting an obstacle closer than 30 cm to the vehicle body.
The Camera360 image processing pipeline shall operate with a processing latency of less than 80ms end-to-end, from raw frame capture to lane and slot boundary output. REQ-005 is a prerequisite.
The parking ECU shall implement a watchdog mechanism with a maximum timeout of 10ms to detect and recover from software faults in the trajectory planning and sensor fusion modules.
All safety-relevant inter-module communication within the parking ECU shall use CRC-32 checksums and sequence numbers to detect data corruption or message loss per AUTOSAR COM specification.
The Summon function shall maintain a geofenced operational boundary of 50 meters from the user's mobile device. The function shall terminate if the vehicle exits this boundary. REQ-006 applies.

The parking HMI shall display the number of parking maneuver steps remaining and the current vehicle position relative to the target slot on the instrument cluster with a refresh rate of at least 10Hz.

The APA system shall support a minimum of 3 consecutive parking maneuver attempts before requiring a system restart, maintaining state consistency across attempts. REQ-003 and REQ-012 must be satisfied.

The parking system shall be capable of operating in parking garages with ceiling heights of at least 1.9 meters without sensor degradation. The Camera360 system shall compensate for low-light conditions below 1 lux.

The Automatic Parking Assist software shall be updatable via OTA (Over-The-Air) mechanism using ISO 14229-compliant UDS diagnostics, with rollback capability to the last known stable version within 5 minutes.

The sensor fusion module shall detect and flag dynamic obstacles (pedestrians, cyclists) moving at speeds above 0.5 m/s within a 5-meter radius of the vehicle using Camera360 and ultrasonic sensor data jointly.

The parking system shall provide a SOTIF (Safety of the Intended Functionality, ISO 21448) analysis for all operational design domains, identifying reasonably foreseeable misuse scenarios for APA and RPA functions.

The parking trajectory controller shall use a model predictive control (MPC) algorithm with a prediction horizon of at least 2 seconds and a control horizon of 0.5 seconds for smooth, curvature-continuous paths.

The Remote Parking Assist user application shall authenticate the user via two-factor authentication before enabling any vehicle motion command. The session key shall expire after 30 minutes of inactivity.

The parking ECU shall perform a self-test (POST — Power-On Self Test) covering all safety-critical components within 2 seconds of ignition-on, reporting any fault to the vehicle fault memory via DTC codes per ISO 14229.

All parking functions shall be disabled when the ambient temperature is below -20°C or above +70°C, as the ultrasonic sensor performance falls outside the validated operational range. REQ-001 depends on this constraint.
